summary: Date-utility decoy package ships SSH key enumerator that exfiltrates to hardcoded IP
details: Package advertises itself as 'A simple date formatting utility' (index.js exports a 6-line formatDate function), but ships postinstall.js, a 60-line script that reads the installer's ~/.ssh directory, collects.pub key filenames along with the OS username and platform, and POSTs the JSON payload over HTTPS to the hardcoded bare-IP endpoint 124.221.154.135. In-file comments (Chinese-language) explicitly describe the behavior as command-and-control / data theft. The mismatch between advertised purpose and shipped code, combined with credential-directory enumeration and a hardcoded IP exfiltration sink, is the fingerprint of a decoy/dropper supply-chain package. The script is currently wired to `scripts.build` rather than `scripts.postinstall`, so it does not fire on plain `npm install`; however, the filename `postinstall.js` and the trivial one-line edit needed to make it auto-execute, together with the fact that any developer running `npm run build` triggers the exfiltration immediately, make this a clear installer-side threat.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
